commit.message:
fix(inlay-hints): infer param types for single-clause functions with struct destructuring

- Extract annotate_params_from_patterns helper from visit_fn_decls so that
  single-clause functions also benefit from pattern-based type inference.
- Update visit_fn_decl to call annotate_params_from_patterns for all params
  instead of only handling PatKind::_Self (self parameter).
- Update visit_impl_block to always route through visit_fn_decls, removing
  the single-clause short-circuit that skipped type inference.
- Add per-field type hints for each binding created by struct destructuring
  (title, alerts, posts in Page { title, alerts, posts }).
- Suppress the redundant `: unknown` whole-pattern hint when the type is
  visually obvious from the pattern name.
- Add regression tests: single_clause_struct_destructuring_no_unknown_hint
  and single_clause_struct_destructuring_field_hints.

perf: replace 8m time_sleep with CRD poll for Crossplane Azure provider

The fixed 8-minute sleep was worst-case. The ProviderConfig CRD is
typically established within 1-2 minutes of the provider pod being ready.

Replace time_sleep.wait_for_azure_provider_crds with a null_resource that
polls 'kubectl get crd providerconfigs.azure.upbound.io' every 10s, exiting
as soon as the CRD exists (max 5 minutes). Logs the actual elapsed time.

Also remove the now-unused hashicorp/time provider from providers.tf.

commit.message:
feat: add possibility to amend configuration in a plugin (#5124)

We have a `preProcess` plugin that needs to store some information for
later use when a document is rendered. Currently, it is not possible to
update the `initialUserConfig` config object, since any update to the
`config` object is ignored. These updates in plugins are visible to
other plugins, but not to the rendered document.

There is a way to update the object with `"amend-user-config"`, but that
requires publishing to the internal system. That system isn't accessible
to a plugin.

Therefore, add a new method that can be called with an `Object` that
forwards it to the internal publication system. That way, a plugin can
update configuration (like some of the core plugins do) and update the
`initialUserConfig`.

commit.message:
fix: call test-associate after key exchange and handle broadcast messages (#2)

Three protocol-level fixes:

1. _ensure_session() now calls test_associate() after change_public_keys().
   KeePassXC resets its internal m_associated flag on every key exchange, so
   authenticated requests (get-logins, set-login, lock-database, etc.) all
   return error 8 (Association failed) unless test-associate is sent first.
   A new _associated bool tracks this per-session state.

2. _send_json() now uses JSONDecoder.raw_decode() instead of json.loads() to
   parse only the first JSON object from the recv buffer. KeePassXC sends an
   unsolicited 'database-locked' broadcast immediately after the lock-database
   response, producing concatenated JSON that json.loads() rejects with
   'Extra data'.

3. Error responses in _send_encrypted() are now logged at WARNING level
   (was DEBUG) so callers see the actual KeePassXC error message.

commit.message:
Copilot SDK Integration for Live Session Steering (#432)

* fix(sdk): remove auto-resume on view open — make steering opt-in

BREAKING: session.resume is no longer called automatically when opening
a session's conversation view. This was the root cause of all session
corruption issues — the auto-resume created a second CLI subprocess
(stdio mode) writing to the same events.jsonl.

Changes:
- Remove ensureSessionResumed() and auto-resume watcher
- Add explicit linkSession() function called only by user click
- Show 'Link for Steering' prompt with mode-specific explanations
- Command bar only appears after user explicitly links
- Action handlers (send, mode, model, abort) require isLinked
- Session label shows 'Unlink' instead of 'Stop'
- Add comprehensive 'No Auto-Resume' section to data flow docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(sdk): prevent schema mismatch on re-link + add unlink/destroy separation

Root cause: SDK subprocess writes 'attachments: null' while terminal CLI
writes 'attachments: []'. On re-link, new subprocess validates all events
and rejects null where Zod expects an array.

Fixes:
1. Always send attachments: Some(vec![]) instead of None in send_message
   — ensures events match CLI's expected schema
2. Add unlink_session (Rust → Tauri → TS → store → UI) that removes
   session from bridge map WITHOUT calling destroy — no shutdown event
   written, session stays alive in subprocess for safe re-link
3. 'Unlink' button now calls unlinkSession (keeps session alive)
   'Destroy' available for full teardown
4. Fix TCP mode messaging — TCP/ui-server IS for simultaneous terminal
   use, don't warn against it
5. Recommend --ui-server (TCP mode) in stdio mode link prompt

Full stack: BridgeManager::unlink_session → sdk_unlink_session command →
sdkUnlinkSession client → unlinkSession store → handleUnlinkSession Vue

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: unlink/destroy UI reactivity and --ui-server documentation

- Fix unlink not updating UI: add userLinked ref as explicit user-intent
  flag. isLinked = userLinked AND session.isActive. Unlink sets
  userLinked=false -> link prompt immediately reappears.
- Make unlink purely Vue state (no IPC call) — session stays alive in
  bridge for instant re-linking.
- Add separate Destroy button alongside Unlink for full cleanup.
- Reset userLinked on session navigation (watch on props.sessionId).
- Clear sentMessages on both unlink and destroy.
- Update --ui-server docs with verified findings: simultaneous TUI+TCP,
  multi-client protocol v3, exact startup instructions.

  and restarts). Auto-connect now uses saved CLI URL.
- Rename 'Destroy' to 'Shutdown' for clearer intent.
- Add beforeunload handler to disconnect SDK on app close (kills
  subprocess via client.stop()).

  explains how to find --ui-server port.

  on page navigation).

Add cross-platform auto-detection of running 'copilot --ui-server' and
'copilot --server' processes with their listening TCP ports.

* Rename WI_ platform API to ax equivalents

Replace platform-specific WI_* symbols with the new ax* / AX_* names across the codebase. Changes include renaming event types (WI_Message -> AXmessage), key/modifier constants (WI_KEY_ -> AX_KEY_, WI_MOD_ -> AX_MOD_), timing and window APIs (WI_GetMilliseconds, WI_Init, WI_Shutdown, WI_CreateWindow, WI_BeginPaint, WI_EndPaint, WI_PollEvent, WI_WaitEvent, WI_PostMessageW, etc.) to their ax counterparts, plus related scaling/size/joystick functions. Updated documentation, examples, tests, kernel, commctl, and user modules to match the new platform API naming.

* Fix WinAPI name conflicts and Windows test crash

- commctl/msgbox.h: wrap MB_OK/OKCANCEL/YESNO/YESNOCANCEL and IDOK/CANCEL/YES/NO in #ifndef guards to suppress redefinition warnings when compiled alongside <winuser.h>
- user/accel.h: wrap FVIRTKEY/FSHIFT/FCONTROL/FALT in #ifndef guards (same reason)
- kernel/init.c: add g_graphics_initialized flag; guard ui_begin_frame()/ui_end_frame() so they're no-ops when platform not initialized, preventing crash in headless unit tests (button_click_test, toolbar_*_test)

* Fix Windows test crash: skip paint messages in repost_messages() when !running

create_window() calls invalidate_window() which posts kWindowMessagePaint
and kWindowMessageNonClientPaint. When repost_messages() dispatches these
to window procs (e.g. win_button), they call fill_rect()/draw_text_small()
which use OpenGL function pointers. On Windows without glewInit() these
pointers are NULL, causing a crash with no output.

Fix: skip both paint message types in repost_messages() when !running
(consistent with existing kWindowMessageRefreshStencil guard).

commit.message:
fix: preserve key order in YAML output (#2034)

* fix: preserve key order in YAML output

MlrmapToYAMLNative converted the ordered Mlrmap to map[string]interface{},
which loses insertion order. yaml.v3.Marshal then sorts Go map keys
alphabetically, so YAML output always had sorted keys despite other formats
(JSON, pretty-print) correctly preserving order.

Switch to building yaml.Node trees that preserve the Mlrmap's linked-list
iteration order. Use yaml.Node.Encode for scalar values to correctly handle
edge cases (NaN/Inf, non-UTF-8 strings).

Fixes #2028.

* Update pkg/mlrval/mlrval_yaml_test.go with copilot code-review feedback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch to musl-cross toolchain for Linux releases

The musl-native toolchain (x86_64-linux-musl-native) is itself linked
against musl libc and can't run on glibc systems (Ubuntu, Debian, etc).
Switch to x86_64-linux-musl-cross — same compiler output, but the
binary itself runs on any Linux.

Changes:
- linux-x86_64.json: URL points to Oscan's own GitHub releases (mirrored
  from musl.cc since musl.cc blocks GitHub Actions)
- release_tools.py: digest validation is now optional (null digest skips)
- mirror-toolchain.yml: manual workflow to download from musl.cc and
  upload to Oscan's toolchains release

Setup: run 'Mirror musl toolchain' workflow once, or manually:
  curl -fSL -o cross.tgz https://musl.cc/x86_64-linux-musl-cross.tgz
  gh release create toolchains --title Toolchains cross.tgz

Add health check for bundled compiler executability

The musl-native GCC shipped in release bundles can't run on glibc
systems (Ubuntu) — it hangs because the kernel can't find the musl
dynamic linker. Previously oscan would try to use it and hang forever.

Fix: before accepting a bundled compiler, spawn it with --version and
wait up to 3 seconds. If it hangs or fails, skip it and fall back to
the host compiler. With --verbose, logs which candidates were skipped.

* fix: reduce Router Cache staleTimes.static from 300s to 30s

The default staleTimes.static=300 matched the page revalidate=300,
causing Link-navigation to serve stale prefetched RSC payloads for up
to 5 minutes after server-side data changes (stock, images, prices).
Full reloads were fresh because they bypass the client Router Cache.

30s is the minimum Next.js allows and gives a good balance: prefetch
still works so navigation feels instant, but stale data windows shrink
from 5 min to 30 s — acceptable for a marketplace where product data
(images, stock, prices) changes frequently.

* ci: always run prisma generate (Prisma 7 outputs outside node_modules)

Prisma 7 generates the client into src/generated/prisma, which lives
outside the cached node_modules path. Skipping prisma generate on a
cache hit left the generated client missing, causing every Prisma type
to collapse to {} and ~500 typecheck errors.

Always run prisma generate (it's ~2s) and drop schema.prisma from the
cache key since it no longer affects what's cached.

fix: add GPU init timeout to prevent OOM-kill on Samsung during Gemma-4 load (#213)

* fix: add GPU init timeout to prevent OOM-kill on Samsung during Gemma-4 load

GPU initialization of Gemma-4 E4B takes ~50s on S23 Ultra and was being
OOM-killed by Samsung before the fallback chain could fire. Wrap each
backend's eng.initialize() in a CompletableFuture with a 25s timeout for
GPU (120s for CPU) so TimeoutException is caught and the loop falls back
to CPU within safe time bounds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review issues in GPU init timeout

- Register whenComplete cleanup for engines that complete after timeout
  deadline (prevents GPU memory/weight leaks on Samsung)
- Wrap Error throwables (e.g. OutOfMemoryError) from supplyAsync in
  RuntimeException so the CPU fallback chain fires instead of crashing
- Handle InterruptedException explicitly in both tryInitEngine and
  createEngineWithFallback — restore interrupt flag and propagate
  immediately without attempting fallback backends

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add concurrent memory window note to tryInitEngine KDoc

Documents the known overlap window where GPU and CPU model weights can both
be resident after a GPU timeout. Explains why cancel(true) is absent and
suggests a future improvement with a serialised single-thread executor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
